passing_score_scale: Percentage (70% passing for all knowledge tests)

testing_centers.provider: PSI Exams (CATS — Computer Assisted Testing Service)
testing_centers.notes: All FAA Airman Knowledge Tests administered at PSI/CATS authorized testing centers. No online proctoring available. Schedule at faa.psiexams.com or call PSI at 1-800-211-2753.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. Student pilot certificate or instructor endorsement required for some tests. Name on ID must match registration exactly.
exam_rules: FAA-approved test supplements provided at testing center. Approved calculators and flight computers permitted. E6B flight computer (mechanical or electronic) allowed. No personal electronic devices. No personal notes or reference materials.
renewal_options: FAA Airman certificates do not expire, but proficiency requirements apply. Airman Knowledge Test result valid for 24 months for scheduling the practical test (checkride). Pilots must complete a Biennial Flight Review (BFR) every 24 months to act as PIC. Currency requirements (landings, instrument approaches) are separate from certificate renewal.

prerequisites.description: Student Pilot Certificate or instructor endorsement required to schedule

retake_policy.notes: 14-day waiting period required after a failed attempt. No limit on total number of attempts. Instructor endorsement or authorized sign-off required before retaking.

official_study_resources.title: FAA Airman Knowledge Test Question Bank
official_study_resources.notes: Official FAA test question database available for free download

official_study_resources.title: FAA Pilot's Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge (PHAK)
official_study_resources.notes: Free PDF download from FAA; primary study resource for Private and Commercial

official_study_resources.title: FAA Instrument Flying Handbook
official_study_resources.notes: Free PDF download from FAA; primary study resource for Instrument Rating

aliases: faa private pilot knowledge test par
